message:
perf(sync/import): skip collision-rename scan when DB is empty

import_from_jsonl unconditionally runs scan_import_collision_renames
to look for issue-id collisions between the JSONL feed and existing
DB records. On a freshly-cloned beads database (or any project where
the DB is empty), there are no existing records to collide with, so
the scan is a guaranteed no-op that still costs a full pass over the
JSONL plus the per-record metadata-map probes.

This commit makes the scan conditional on the import-metadata maps
being non-empty:

  * ImportMetadataMaps gains an `is_empty()` helper that returns true
    only when meta_by_id, id_by_ext_ref, and id_by_hash are all empty
    (so any populated map still triggers the full scan — we cannot
    short-circuit if the DB has even one record we might collide with).
  * ImportValidationPlan gains an `ephemeral_count: usize` field, and
    collect_import_validation_plan increments it whenever the
    validated issue carries `issue.ephemeral = true`.
  * import_from_jsonl now branches: if metadata.is_empty(), the
    collision-rename pass is skipped, and the previously-counted
    ephemeral records are credited to result.skipped_count so the
    downstream summary still reflects "X records skipped because they
    were ephemeral" instead of silently dropping them. Otherwise the
    original scan_import_collision_renames call runs unchanged.

Net effect: first-time imports into an empty .beads/beads.db avoid an
unnecessary full-JSONL scan and the per-record HashMap lookups it
performs, while warm imports (the common case once a project has any
prior issue) take exactly the same code path as before.
